2604.24444.title: Can You Make It Sound Like You? Post-Editing LLM-Generated Text for Personal Style
2604.24444.paper_summary: Despite the growing use of large language models (LLMs) for writing tasks, users may hesitate to rely on LLMs when personal style is important. Post-editing LLM-generated drafts or translations is a common collaborative writing strategy, but it remains unclear whether users can effectively reshape LLM-generated text to reflect their personal style. We conduct a pre-registered online study ($n=81$) in which participants post-edit LLM-generated drafts for writing tasks where personal style matters to them. Using embedding-based style similarity metrics, we find that post-editing increases stylistic similarity to participants' unassisted writing and reduces similarity to fully LLM-generated output. However, post-edited text still remains stylistically closer in style to LLM text than to participants' unassisted control text, and it exhibits reduced stylistic diversity compared to unassisted human text. We find a gap between perceived stylistic authenticity and model-measured stylistic similarity, with post-edited text often perceived as representative of participants' personal style despite remaining detectable LLM stylistic traces.
2604.24444.paper_summary_zh: 儘管大型語言模型（LLMs）在寫作任務中的使用日益增長，但當個人風格重要時，用戶可能會猶豫依賴LLMs。對LLM生成的草稿或翻譯進行後編輯是一種常見的協作寫作策略，但尚不清楚用戶是否能有效地重塑LLM生成的文本以反映他們的個人風格。我們進行了一項預註冊的線上研究（$n=81$），參與者對於對他們來說重要的寫作任務進行LLM生成草稿的後編輯。使用基於嵌入的風格相似性指標，我們發現後編輯增加了與參與者未經協助寫作的風格相似性，並減少了與完全LLM生成輸出的相似性。然而，後編輯的文本在風格上仍然比參與者未經協助的控制文本更接近LLM文本，並且與未經協助的人類文本相比，顯示出較低的風格多樣性。我們發現感知的風格真實性與模型測量的風格相似性之間存在差距，後編輯的文本經常被認為是參與者個人風格的代表，儘管仍然可以檢測到LLM的風格痕跡。
2604.24444.author: Connor Baumler et.al.
2604.24444.authors: Connor Baumler, Calvin Bao, Huy Nghiem, Xinchen Yang, Marine Carpuat, Hal Daumé
